cards.definition: family — les parents (parents), les frères et sœurs (siblings), les grands-parents (grandparents)
marriage — se marier (to get married), le/la marié(e) (bride/groom)
divorce — divorcer (to get divorced), séparé(e) (separated)
friendship — un/une ami(e) (a friend), être ami avec (to be friends with)
relationships — sortir avec (to go out with), s'entendre bien avec (to get on well with)
chores — faire la vaisselle (do the dishes), passer l'aspirateur (vacuum), faire son lit (make your bed)
breakfast — usually light in France: bread, croissants, café or orange juice
lunch — traditionally the main meal in France, often two to three courses
dinner — evening meal, family eat together, often with bread and cheese
habits/routines — se lever (get up), se coucher (go to bed), prendre une douche (have a shower)

questions.question: What does 'les parents' mean?
questions.explanation: Les parents = parents. Les grands-parents = grandparents. Les proches or la famille = relatives.

questions.question: What does 'se marier' mean?
questions.explanation: Se marier = to get married. Je me marie = I am getting married. Ils se sont mariés = They got married.

questions.question: What does 's'entendre bien avec' mean?
options: B) To get on well with
questions.explanation: S'entendre bien avec = to get on well with. Je m'entends bien avec ma sœur = I get on well with my sister.

questions.question: What does 'faire la vaisselle' mean?
questions.explanation: Faire la vaisselle = to do the dishes. Other chores: faire son lit (make bed), passer l'aspirateur (vacuum).

questions.question: What is 'le déjeuner'?
questions.explanation: Le déjeuner = lunch. Le petit-déjeuner = breakfast. Le dîner = dinner/evening meal.

questions.question: What does 'se coucher' mean?
questions.explanation: Se coucher = to go to bed. Se lever = to get up. Both are reflexive verbs.

questions.question: What is 'l'amitié'?
questions.explanation: L'amitié = friendship. Un(e) ami(e) = a friend. Mon/ma meilleur(e) ami(e) = my best friend.

questions.question: What does 'sortir avec' mean?
options: B) To go out with (in a relationship)
questions.explanation: Sortir avec = to go out with / to date. Je sors avec quelqu'un = I am going out with someone.

questions.question: What is 'le dîner'?
questions.explanation: Le dîner = dinner. In France, the family typically eats together in the evening with bread and cheese.

questions.question: What does 'prendre une douche' mean?
questions.explanation: Prendre une douche = to have a shower. Prendre un bain = to have a bath.

questions.question: Les parents means grandparents in French.
questions.explanation: Les parents = parents. Les grands-parents = grandparents.

questions.question: Se marier means to get divorced.
questions.explanation: Se marier = to get married. Divorcer = to get divorced.

questions.question: S'entendre bien avec means to get on well with.
questions.explanation: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère = I get on well with my brother.

questions.question: Le déjeuner is the evening meal.
questions.explanation: Le déjeuner = lunch. Le dîner = dinner (evening meal).

questions.question: Faire la vaisselle means to do the dishes.
questions.explanation: Faire la vaisselle is one of the most common chores — don't confuse with faire la cuisine (to cook).

questions.question: Se coucher means to get up in the morning.
questions.explanation: Se coucher = to go to bed. Se lever = to get up.

questions.question: L'amitié means friendship.
questions.explanation: L'amitié = friendship. Un(e) ami(e) = a friend.

questions.question: Sortir avec means to go shopping.
questions.explanation: Sortir avec = to go out with (in a relationship). Faire des courses = to go shopping.

questions.question: Le petit-déjeuner is the main meal in France.
questions.explanation: Le déjeuner (lunch) is traditionally the main meal. Le petit-déjeuner = breakfast.

questions.question: Prendre une douche means to have a shower.
questions.explanation: Prendre une douche = to have a shower. Prendre un bain = to have a bath.

questions.question: La ________ = family.
questions.explanation: La famille includes les parents, les frères et sœurs, and les grands-parents.

questions.question: Se ________ = to get married.
questions.explanation: Je veux me marier un jour = I want to get married one day.

questions.question: S'entendre ________ avec = to get on well with.
questions.explanation: Je m'entends bien avec mes parents = I get on well with my parents.

questions.question: Faire la ________ = to do the dishes.
questions.explanation: Les corvées = chores. Faire la vaisselle is a common household chore.

questions.question: Le ________ = lunch.
questions.explanation: In France, lunch is traditionally an important, often two-course meal.

questions.question: Se ________ = to go to bed.
questions.explanation: Je me couche à dix heures = I go to bed at ten o'clock.

questions.question: L'________ = friendship.
questions.explanation: L'amitié est très importante pour moi = Friendship is very important to me.

questions.question: ________ avec = to go out with (in a relationship).
questions.explanation: Elle sort avec un garçon de sa classe = She is going out with a boy from her class.

questions.question: Le ________ = dinner/evening meal.
questions.explanation: On dîne ensemble chaque soir = We have dinner together every evening.

questions.question: Prendre une ________ = to have a shower.
questions.explanation: Je prends une douche chaque matin = I have a shower every morning.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

cards.definition: holidays — partir en vacances (to go on holiday), passer du temps (to spend time)
hotel — réserver une chambre (to book a room), la chambre (room), l'ascenseur (lift)
beach — se baigner (to swim), faire du surf (to surf), prendre un bain de soleil (to sunbathe)
transport — le train (train), l'avion (plane), la voiture (car), le vélo (bike)
train station — le quai (platform), l'horaire (timetable), le billet (ticket)
airport — l'enregistrement (check-in), le vol (flight), la porte (gate)
to go sightseeing — visiter (to visit), un monument (monument), une cathédrale (cathedral)
camping — une tente (tent), un sac de couchage (sleeping bag)
activities — faire de la randonnée (hiking), faire du vélo (cycling), faire de la voile (sailing)
the weather — il fait beau (it's nice), il pleut (it's raining), il neige (it's snowing), il fait chaud (it's hot)

questions.question: What does 'partir en vacances' mean?
options: A) To come back from holiday
questions.explanation: Partir en vacances = to go on holiday. Je pars en vacances demain = I am going on holiday tomorrow.

questions.question: What is 'la gare'?
questions.explanation: La gare = train station. L'aéroport = airport. La gare routière = bus station.

questions.question: What does 'il fait beau' mean?
questions.explanation: Il fait beau = it's nice / the weather is good. Il fait mauvais = it's bad weather.

questions.question: What does 'faire de la randonnée' mean?
questions.explanation: Faire de la randonnée = to go hiking. Faire du vélo = cycling. Faire de la voile = sailing.

questions.question: What is 'l'enregistrement' at an airport?
questions.explanation: L'enregistrement = check-in. La porte = gate. Le vol = flight.

questions.question: What does 'réserver une chambre' mean?
options: A) To check out of a hotel
questions.explanation: Réserver une chambre = to book a room. Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux personnes = I would like to book a room for two people.

questions.question: What does 'se baigner' mean?
questions.explanation: Se baigner = to swim / to bathe. On the beach: se baigner (swim), prendre un bain de soleil (sunbathe).

questions.question: What is 'le camping'?
questions.explanation: Le camping = camping. Une tente = a tent. Un sac de couchage = a sleeping bag.

questions.question: What does 'il neige' mean?
questions.explanation: Il neige = it is snowing. Il pleut = it is raining. Il gèle = it is freezing.

questions.question: What does 'faire du tourisme' mean?
options: A) To go on a tour bus
questions.explanation: Faire du tourisme = to go sightseeing. Visiter = to visit. Un monument = a monument/sight.

questions.question: Partir en vacances means to return from holiday.
questions.explanation: Partir en vacances = to go on holiday. Rentrer = to return/come back.

questions.question: La gare is a train station.
questions.explanation: La gare = train station. Le quai = platform. L'horaire = timetable. Le billet = ticket.

questions.question: Il fait chaud means it is cold.
questions.explanation: Il fait chaud = it is hot. Il fait froid = it is cold.

questions.question: Faire de la randonnée means to go hiking.
questions.explanation: Faire de la randonnée = to go hiking. C'est une activité populaire en montagne = It's a popular mountain activity.

questions.question: L'aéroport means the train station.
questions.explanation: L'aéroport = airport. La gare = train station.

questions.question: Se baigner means to sunbathe.
questions.explanation: Se baigner = to swim/bathe. Prendre un bain de soleil = to sunbathe.

questions.question: Il pleut means it is raining.
questions.explanation: Il pleut = it is raining. Il pleuvait = it was raining (imperfect).

questions.question: Réserver une chambre means to book a room.
questions.explanation: Réserver = to book/reserve. Je voudrais réserver une chambre = I would like to book a room.

questions.question: Le camping involves staying in a tent.
questions.explanation: Le camping = camping. Une tente = tent. Un sac de couchage = sleeping bag.

questions.question: Faire du tourisme means to work in a tourist office.
questions.explanation: Faire du tourisme = to go sightseeing/be a tourist.

questions.question: Partir en ________ = to go on holiday.
questions.explanation: Je pars en vacances en été = I go on holiday in summer.

questions.question: La ________ = train station.
questions.explanation: Je vais à la gare pour prendre le train = I go to the station to catch the train.

questions.question: Il fait ________ = the weather is nice.
questions.explanation: Il fait beau aujourd'hui = The weather is nice today.

questions.question: Faire de la ________ = to go hiking.
questions.explanation: J'aime faire de la randonnée en montagne = I love hiking in the mountains.

questions.question: L'________ = airport.
questions.explanation: On va à l'aéroport en taxi = We go to the airport by taxi.

questions.question: Réserver une ________ = to book a room.
questions.explanation: Je voudrais réserver une chambre pour deux nuits = I would like to book a room for two nights.

questions.question: Se ________ = to swim/bathe.
questions.explanation: On peut se baigner à la plage = You can swim at the beach.

questions.question: Il ________ = it is snowing.
questions.explanation: Il neige en hiver dans les Alpes = It snows in winter in the Alps.

questions.question: Faire du ________ = to go sightseeing.
questions.explanation: J'aime faire du tourisme quand je suis en vacances = I like sightseeing when I'm on holiday.

questions.question: La ________ = the weather.
questions.explanation: La météo dit qu'il va pleuvoir demain = The forecast says it will rain tomorrow.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: holidays — partir en vacances = to go on holiday
weather — il fait beau, il pleut, il neige

cards.definition: secondary school (age 11–15) in France — equivalent to Key Stage 3 and the start of GCSE
upper secondary school (age 15–18) in France — students take le baccalauréat
subjects — les maths, les sciences, l'histoire-géographie, les langues vivantes
the French equivalent of A-levels — taken at the end of lycée, age 17–18
school uniform — France does not generally require uniforms unlike the UK
homework — faire ses devoirs (to do homework), rendre un devoir (to hand in work)
school canteen — manger à la cantine (to eat in the canteen)
school hours — French school often runs 8am–5pm with a long lunch break
to succeed / to pass — réussir un examen (to pass an exam)
to fail — échouer à un examen (to fail an exam)

questions.question: What is 'le lycée'?
options: B) Secondary school for 11-15 year olds
C) Upper secondary school for 15-18 year olds
questions.explanation: Le lycée is French upper secondary education (15–18). Students study for le baccalauréat. Le collège = 11–15 year olds.

questions.question: What does 'faire ses devoirs' mean?
questions.explanation: Faire ses devoirs = to do homework. Rendre un devoir = to hand in homework.

questions.question: What is 'la cantine'?
questions.explanation: La cantine = the school canteen. Manger à la cantine = to eat in the canteen.

questions.question: What does 'réussir un examen' mean?
options: C) To revise for an exam
questions.explanation: Réussir = to succeed / to pass. Réussir un examen = to pass an exam. Échouer = to fail.

questions.question: What is 'le baccalauréat'?
options: A) A French middle school exam
B) The French equivalent of A-levels
C) A primary school leaving certificate
questions.explanation: Le baccalauréat (le bac) is taken at the end of lycée — it is the French school leaving qualification, equivalent to A-levels.

questions.question: What does 'les matières' mean?
questions.explanation: Les matières = school subjects. Les matières obligatoires = compulsory subjects. Les matières facultatives = optional subjects.

questions.question: What does 'échouer à un examen' mean?
options: D) To cheat in an exam
questions.explanation: Échouer = to fail. Échouer à un examen = to fail an exam. The opposite is réussir un examen.

questions.question: Does France generally require school uniform?
options: A) Yes — it is compulsory in all schools
B) No — French schools generally do not have uniforms
questions.explanation: Unlike the UK, most French schools do not require uniforms — though some private schools may have dress codes.

questions.question: What is 'le collège' in France?
options: C) Lower secondary school for 11–15 year olds
questions.explanation: Le collège = lower secondary school (11–15) in France. It covers the equivalent of UK Year 7 to Year 10.

questions.question: What does 'rendre un devoir' mean?
questions.explanation: Rendre un devoir = to hand in/submit homework or coursework.

questions.question: Le collège in France is for students aged 15–18.
questions.explanation: Le collège is for 11–15 year olds. Le lycée is for 15–18 year olds.

questions.question: Faire ses devoirs means to do homework.
questions.explanation: Faire ses devoirs = to do homework. An essential phrase for school topic answers.

questions.question: La cantine is the school library.
questions.explanation: La cantine = the school canteen. La bibliothèque = the library.

questions.question: Réussir un examen means to fail an exam.
questions.explanation: Réussir = to succeed / pass. Échouer = to fail.

questions.question: Le baccalauréat is the French equivalent of A-levels.
questions.explanation: Le bac is taken at the end of lycée (age 17–18) and is required for university entrance.

questions.question: French schools generally require students to wear uniform.
questions.explanation: Most French schools do not require school uniform — unlike most British schools.

questions.question: Les matières means school subjects.
questions.explanation: Les matières includes maths, sciences, langues vivantes, histoire-géographie, etc.

questions.question: Échouer means to succeed in an exam.
questions.explanation: Échouer = to fail. Réussir = to succeed/pass.

questions.question: French school days often run from 8am to 5pm.
questions.explanation: French school hours are typically longer than British ones, with a long lunch break (often 2 hours) in the middle.

questions.question: Rendre un devoir means to do homework.
questions.explanation: Rendre un devoir = to hand in work. Faire ses devoirs = to do homework.

questions.question: Le ________ = lower secondary school in France (age 11–15).
questions.explanation: Le collège is equivalent to Key Stage 3 and the start of GCSE in England.

questions.question: Le ________ = upper secondary school in France (age 15–18).
questions.explanation: At the lycée, students work towards le baccalauréat.

questions.question: Faire ses ________ = to do homework.
questions.explanation: C'est important de faire ses devoirs tous les soirs = It's important to do homework every evening.

questions.question: La ________ = the school canteen.
questions.explanation: Je mange à la cantine tous les jours = I eat in the canteen every day.

questions.question: ________ un examen = to pass an exam.
questions.explanation: J'espère réussir tous mes examens = I hope to pass all my exams.

questions.question: Le ________ is the French school leaving qualification, equivalent to A-levels.
questions.explanation: Le bac is essential for university entry in France.

questions.question: Les ________ = school subjects.
questions.explanation: Mes matières préférées sont les sciences et les langues = My favourite subjects are sciences and languages.

questions.question: ________ à un examen = to fail an exam.
questions.explanation: Je ne veux pas échouer à mes examens = I don't want to fail my exams.

questions.question: ________ un devoir = to hand in homework/coursework.
questions.explanation: Il faut rendre ce devoir vendredi = This homework must be handed in on Friday.

questions.question: L'________ scolaire = school uniform (not generally worn in France).
questions.explanation: En France, on ne porte pas souvent d'uniforme scolaire = In France, school uniform is not often worn.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: to fail — échouer à un examen

cards.definition: the present tense — used for current actions and general truths — je mange, il parle, nous allons
the perfect tense — used for completed past actions — j'ai mangé (I ate), je suis allé(e) (I went)
the imperfect tense — used for repeated/ongoing past actions or descriptions — c'était, j'allais, il faisait
the near future — aller + infinitive — je vais manger (I am going to eat)
the simple future — used for future plans — je mangerai (I will eat), il ira (he will go)
the conditional — would — je voudrais (I would like), j'aimerais (I would love)
the auxiliary verbs avoir and être — used to form the passé composé
reflexive verbs — action done to yourself — se lever (to get up), se laver (to wash)
adjectives — must agree with the noun in gender and number — grand/grande/grands/grandes
negation — ne...pas (not), ne...jamais (never), ne...plus (no longer), ne...rien (nothing)

questions.question: How do you form the futur proche?
questions.explanation: Futur proche = aller (present) + infinitive. Je vais manger = I am going to eat. Nous allons partir = We are going to leave.

questions.question: Which auxiliary verb is used with 'aller' in the passé composé?
questions.explanation: Most movement verbs (including aller) use être in the passé composé. Je suis allé(e) = I went. The past participle agrees with the subject.

questions.question: What is the imparfait used for?
options: B) Completed single actions in the past
C) Ongoing/repeated past actions and descriptions
questions.explanation: The imparfait is used for: ongoing past actions (j'allais = I was going), repeated habits (je mangeais = I used to eat), and descriptions (il faisait beau = it was nice).

questions.question: What does 'je voudrais' mean?
questions.explanation: Je voudrais = I would like. It is the conditional of vouloir. Polite and used for requests.

questions.question: What is the rule for adjective agreement in French?
options: B) Adjectives change only for plural
C) Adjectives change to match the gender and number of the noun
D) Adjectives come before the noun always
questions.explanation: French adjectives must agree in gender (masculine/feminine) and number (singular/plural) with the noun they describe. Grand → grande → grands → grandes.

questions.question: How do you say 'I never go' in French?
questions.explanation: Ne...jamais = never. Je ne vais jamais = I never go. Remember: ne before verb, jamais after.

questions.question: What are reflexive verbs?
options: A) Verbs used only in formal French
B) Verbs where the action is done to yourself — se lever, se laver
C) Irregular verbs with unpredictable forms
D) Verbs that use avoir in the passé composé
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs use reflexive pronouns (me, te, se, nous, vous): je me lève = I get up. Je me lave = I wash (myself).

questions.question: What is the futur simple of 'manger'?
questions.explanation: Futur simple: take the infinitive + endings (-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont). Je mangerai = I will eat.

questions.question: What does 'ne...plus' mean?
questions.explanation: Ne...plus = no longer / not anymore. Je ne mange plus de viande = I no longer eat meat.

questions.question: What is the passé composé of 'finir' (to finish)?
questions.explanation: Passé composé of -ir verbs: avoir + past participle (-i). J'ai fini = I finished / I have finished.

questions.question: Futur proche uses aller + infinitive.
questions.explanation: Je vais faire mes devoirs = I am going to do my homework.

questions.question: All verbs use avoir in the passé composé.
questions.explanation: Movement verbs and reflexive verbs use être. DR MRS VANDERTRAMP verbs use être.

questions.question: The imparfait is used for completed single past actions.
questions.explanation: The passé composé is for completed single actions. The imparfait is for ongoing, habitual, or descriptive past.

questions.question: Je voudrais is the conditional — meaning 'I would like'.
questions.explanation: Je voudrais is polite and essential — used constantly for requests and preferences.

questions.question: French adjectives agree in gender and number with the noun.
questions.explanation: Grand (masc sg) → grande (fem sg) → grands (masc pl) → grandes (fem pl).

questions.question: Ne...jamais means 'not anymore'.
questions.explanation: Ne...jamais = never. Ne...plus = not anymore / no longer.

questions.question: Reflexive verbs use reflexive pronouns (me, te, se, nous, vous, se).
questions.explanation: Je me lève à sept heures = I get up at seven o'clock.

questions.question: The futur simple of regular -er verbs adds endings to the infinitive.
questions.explanation: Je mangerai, tu mangeras, il mangera, nous mangerons, vous mangerez, ils mangeront.

questions.question: Adjectives in French always come before the noun.
questions.explanation: Most French adjectives come AFTER the noun. A few common ones (beau, grand, petit, jeune, vieux) come before.

questions.question: Ne...rien means 'not anything / nothing'.
questions.explanation: Je ne mange rien = I eat nothing / I don't eat anything.

questions.question: Futur proche = ________ + infinitive.
questions.explanation: Je vais partir demain = I am going to leave tomorrow.

questions.question: Passé composé = auxiliary (avoir or ________) + past participle.
questions.explanation: Être is used with movement verbs and reflexive verbs.

questions.question: The ________ is used for ongoing or repeated past actions and descriptions.
questions.explanation: Quand j'étais jeune, j'allais à la piscine = When I was young, I used to go to the pool.

questions.question: Je ________ = I would like (conditional of vouloir).
questions.explanation: Je voudrais une chambre pour deux personnes = I would like a room for two people.

questions.question: French adjectives agree in ________ and number with the noun.
questions.explanation: Grand (masc) → grande (fem). Grands (masc pl) → grandes (fem pl).

questions.question: Ne...________ = never.
questions.explanation: Je ne mens jamais = I never lie.

questions.question: Ne...________ = no longer / not anymore.
questions.explanation: Je ne mange plus de viande = I no longer eat meat.

questions.question: ________ verbs use reflexive pronouns — se lever, se laver, se coucher.
questions.explanation: Je me lève = I get up. Tu te laves = You wash. Il se couche = He goes to bed.

questions.question: Futur simple of -er verbs = infinitive + ________.
questions.explanation: Je parlerai = I will speak. Tu parleras = You will speak.

questions.question: Ne...________ = nothing / not anything.
questions.explanation: Il ne fait rien = He does nothing.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: current actions — je mange, il parle
completed past actions — j'ai mangé, je suis allé
